name: me-and-white-supremacy
description: Layla F. Saad's 'Me and White Supremacy: Combat Racism, Change the World, and Become a Good Ancestor' — a 28-day personal antiracism workbook for people with white privilege to examine their complicity in white supremacy. Covers privilege, fragility, tone policing, cultural appropriation, allyship, and actionable commitments for becoming a good ancestor.
useCase: Understanding white supremacy as a system, examining personal complicity through daily journaling, developing antiracist practices, learning about privilege and fragility, and building the stamina to do sustained antiracism work.
knownRisks: Challenging and uncomfortable for white readers — requires vulnerability
Not a scholarly work — a practical workbook
Some readers may feel the framework is too blunt or confrontational
The work is ongoing — 28 days is just the beginning
